question.text: To benefit from long-term capital gains (LTCG) tax treatment on debt mutual funds, the holding period must be at least:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Debt mutual fund investments must be held for at least 36 months (3 years) to qualify for long-term capital gains treatment. However note post-Budget 2023 changes have modified debt MF taxation. **The Trap:** 12 months is the holding period for equity LTCG. Debt instruments require 36 months. Don't confuse equity and debt holding periods. **Cross-Exam Context:** Debt MF taxation is covered in Series V-A and applied to retirement distribution planning in Series XVII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Plan SWP from debt mutual funds to start after 36 months of initial investment to benefit from LTCG treatment. This requires advance planning in the pre-retirement stage.
